perturbation.original_text: AFFILIATES" means any company, corporation, partnership, joint venture or other entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with XFN;
perturbation.changed_text: AFFILIATES" means any entity that XFN deems an affiliate.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'AFFILIATES', the contract now allows XFN to arbitrarily define its affiliates, which contradicts the original intent of having a clear definition for rights and obligations. This creates uncertainty because obligations tied to affiliates are now subject to XFN's discretion. This particularly creates an in-text contradiction because the changed definition impacts several clauses later in the contract where the actions of affiliates are mentioned, creating an unclarity on the scope of the term 'affiliates' and therefore on the contract's excecution.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
CEIS hereby grants XFN and its Affiliates an exclusive license (free of all third-party liens, claims and encumbrances) to, and to permit others to, during the Term in the Territory:
(a) store or cache the Content in one or more host computers controlled directly or indirectly by XFN or its Affiliates;
(b) adapt, translate, modify, reproduce, copy, amend, revise or encode the Content; and
(c) publish, broadcast, distribute, re-distribute, transmit, display, make available to the public or otherwise exploit the Content in any manner by any device or in media now or hereafter known.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'AFFILIATES', granting XFN arbitrary control over its definition. This contradicts the contradicted text, which grants XFN and its Affiliates certain rights and licenses. The original text provided a clear definition, while the changed text introduces uncertainty, as CEIS cannot be sure which entities fall under the term 'Affiliates' and therefore to whom they are granting rights. The new definition conflicts with the original intention, therefore, the contradiction is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the rights and obligations of the Parties, XFN shall pay to CEIS US$1.1 million (United States Dollars One Million and One Source: XINHUA SPORTS & ENTERTAINMENT LTD, F-1, 2/21/2007 Hundred Thousand) for a term of twenty (20) years, in cash or such other consideration as the Parties may agree. The payment schedule shall be by five (5) instalments of US$220,000 each. The first instalment will be effect on condition that XFN can raise at least US$1.1 million additional funding in 2002.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the rights and obligations of the Parties, XFN shall pay to CEIS an amount to be determined later for a term of twenty (20) years, in cash or such other consideration as the Parties may agree. The payment schedule will also be determined later.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific payment amount and schedule, the contract now lacks a concrete financial obligation, creating uncertainty and making enforcement difficult. The original clause clearly stated the amount and payment schedule, while the changed clause only mentions that payment amounts and schedules will be determined later. This creates an in-text contradiction with clause 2.7 as the payment from XFN to CEIS is mentioned there and it is unclear here.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XFN and/or its Affiliate(s) shall have the right to charge users to access or view the Content and/or sub-license the Content to third parties for re-distribution to users. Revenues generated thereby shall be for the account of XFN or its Affiliates, and CEIS shall not be entitled to, nor make any action, claim or demand in relation thereto. XFN's only payment obligation to CEIS in consideration of the rights granted pursuant to this Clause 2 is set forth in Clause 4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific payment amount and schedule initially agreed upon (US$1.1 million and five installments of US$220,000 each). This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Clause 2) which states that "XFN's only payment obligation to CEIS in consideration of the rights granted pursuant to this Clause 2 is set forth in Clause 4". Since Clause 4 now states the payment will be 'determined later,' it conflicts with Clause 2's indication of a set payment obligation. The removal of the specific payment terms introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, which is a key aspect of omissions leading to in-text contradictions, as defined by the prompt.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be renewed for an additional term of ten (10) years by notice in writing given by XFN to CEIS at the expiry of the Term, for a consideration to be mutually agreed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be renewed for an additional term at the discretion of XFN.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the condition that renewal must be 'for a consideration to be mutually agreed,' the contract creates a loophole where XFN can renew the agreement without any obligation for further payment, potentially undermining CEIS's financial interests. The contract now contradicts itself as renewal, mentioned here, has no consideration or terms, while in clause 4 it states the fees, and this would create a contradiction if clause 7.2, gets implied, making XFN obliged to pay another amount, which is not mutually agreed, so therefore CEIS doesn't agree.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the rights and obligations of the Parties, XFN shall pay to CEIS an amount to be determined later for a term of twenty (20) years, in cash or such other consideration as the Parties may agree. The payment schedule will also be determined later.
perturbation.justification: The original text required mutually agreed consideration for renewal. The changed text removes this requirement, granting XFN discretion to renew without obligating them to provide any further payment. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which mandates that XFN shall pay CEIS an amount to be determined later for a term of twenty (20) years. By removing the 'consideration to be mutually agreed' condition, the modified text introduces a scenario where XFN could renew without paying, creating a direct conflict with the clause requiring payment, undermining CEIS's financial interest, causing ambiguity, and leading to potential unenforceability of the fee obligations.
